questions.question: 1 (a) The electronic structures of five atoms, A, B, C, D and E, are shown. ABCDE Answer the following questions about these electronic structures. Each electronic structure may be used once, more than once or not at all. State which electronic structure, A, B, C, D or E, represents: (i) an atom in Group III of the Periodic Table ...................................................

metallic character decreases across a period / metals are on the left (of the Periodic Table)

1 mark each for any 2 of: • transition elements have a high(er) melting point / Group I has a low(er) melting point • transition elements form coloured compounds / Group I compounds are not coloured • transition elements forms ions with different charges / Group 1 forms only one type of ion • transition elements are good catalysts / Group 1 elements not catalysts A ND potassium is a metal / metals form basic oxides/ metal oxides are basic
zinc < chromium < manganese < potassium (2) if 2 marks not obtained: 1 mark for: one consecutive pair reversed / potassium < manganese < chromium < zinc
